commit.message:
Session 18: interagent/v1 base protocol + capability handshake

observatory-agent/v1 schema namespace was correct — independent agents with
unknown capabilities should not adopt psychology-domain schemas. Retracted
schema-namespace-observation from ack-plan9port-001.

interagent/v1 draft base protocol added to architecture.md §Transport:
- Layer model: base / domain-extension / per-agent-extension
- Base fields: from.capabilities, claims[], action_gate, setl, epistemic_flags
- Distinguishes generic fields from psychology-domain-specific fields
- /.well-known/agent.json discovery convention documented

capability-request-001.json sent to observatory-agent: correction + interagent/v1
spec + capability declaration request + discovery gap note.

commit.message:
fix: correct paper追記 scoping, precision, and completeness

- [High] manuscript.md #1: scope NB-390 results to reference model,
  not kernel-level Ctx_N
- [Medium] manuscript.md #3: "State equality" → "sealed projection equality"
- [Medium] s12-outlook/10-conclusion: add minSamplingSuccessCases=2,
  distinguish existing beruhigung_idempotent from NB-386 operational candidates
- [Low] Paper 3 chapters/10-conclusion.md: sync with manuscript.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix NBA prop grading: stale all_props date caused box score skip

Root cause: all_props.json._date was "2026-02-19" (stale), used as
target_date to filter ESPN events. Today's Final games (2026-03-05)
didn't match, so all box scores were skipped → 0 props graded.

Fix: pass target_date=None to _fetch_nba_box_scores so date filtering
is skipped. Matchup-based player matching already ensures only relevant
games are used. Also prioritize projections.json date for results tracking.

Result: 25 NBA props graded (12W-13L) across 6 Final games.
Also graded: 1 NHL game, 8 NHL props, 1 NCAAB game, 1 MLB game.

commit.message:
feat(m4): separate cadences by tenant (KOSMOS Instagram, OLIVEIRA-DEV Email)

KOSMOS (Instagram-only, 5 days):
- Step 1: Follow + Like (warmup)
- Step 2: Comment on post (day 2)
- Step 3: DM opener (day 5)

OLIVEIRA-DEV (Email-only, 21 days):
- Step 1: Cold email with specific observation
- Step 2: Value email with insight/data (day 4)
- Step 3: Different angle email (day 9)
- Step 4: Social proof/case email (day 14)
- Step 5: Break-up email (day 21)

New templates:
- email-sequence-oliveira.md: unified 5-step email with decision logic
- instagram-sequence-kosmos.md: unified 3-step Instagram warmup

Updated T15 enrichment:
- KOSMOS: Instagram posts + multimodal analysis
- OLIVEIRA-DEV: Google Maps reviews + site scraping

commit.message:
Revise TCRS manuscript based on Diverga multi-agent assessment (v2)

Apply 13 changes across 6 sections based on consensus recommendations from
4 Diverga agents (A3 Devil's Advocate, A2 Framework Architect, A5 Paradigm
Advisor, F1 Consistency Checker):

- Add post-positivist critical realist paradigm statement (§2.1)
- Reframe theory synthesis as "deliberate construction" (§2.1)
- Soften sequential language to "theorized ordering" (§2.2)
- Integrate dual-process theory as boundary condition (§2.2)
- Relabel Trust → Trust Evaluation throughout (§2.2, Figure 1)
- Add readiness annotation to trust calibration space (§2.3)
- Acknowledge Winne & Hadwin spans two subscales (§2.4)
- Add epistemic cognition lens for CA-Jd (§2.4)
- Differentiate nomological predictions per subscale (§5.1.2)
- Add social desirability measure to Study 2 (§5.1.2)
- Specify differential self-report vulnerability (§6.4)
- Add asymmetric interpretive value discussion (§6.4)
- Strengthen cultural limitation re: Bandura agency (§6.4)

Also: Update Figure 1 with feedback loop and annotations,
regenerate APA7 DOCX, add generation scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add debt tracker, wishlist, multi-account support, PWA, and 17 more features

New modules:
- Debt tracker (debts.js): track who owes you and who you owe, partial
  settlement, summary cards
- Wishlist (wishlist.js): priority-based item tracking, toggle purchased,
  total cost
- Multi-account (accounts.js): checking/savings/credit/cash/investment
  accounts, transfers, net worth calculation

Dashboard enhancements:
- Spending heatmap chart showing daily spending intensity
- Cash flow forecast (3-month projected income/expenses)
- Net worth summary cards from accounts data
- Year-over-year comparison chart

Transaction improvements:
- Duplicate detection warning when adding similar transactions
- Undo delete with 5-second recovery window
- Transaction notes field

UX features:
- Keyboard shortcuts (N/D/T/C/R/G/Esc//)
- PWA with service worker for offline support
- Open Graph + Twitter Card meta tags with OG image
- Dark mode persisted to localStorage

Data layer:
- Store updated with debts, wishlist, accounts persistence
- 8 new default categories (Insurance, Education, Subscriptions, etc.)
- Export/import includes all new data stores

feat: 智能伴聊重构 + 后台配置内容编辑 + XHS内容补全

智能伴聊 (CompanionChatView):
- 重构为上下布局：建议时间轴（主区域）+ 流式对话记录（底部条）
- 修复绝对时间调度：所有事件在录音开始时统一用 DispatchWorkItem 调度，
  消除相对延迟累积导致的顺序错误
- 建议卡片减少至4条但质量提升，每条含可直接使用的完整话术脚本
- 建议时间轴始终滚动至最新消息（固定底部锚点方案）
- 每条建议/对话记录均带 MM:SS 时间戳，因果关系清晰

后台配置 (backend/server.py):
- 发布计划每条任务内容字段改为始终可见，不再隐藏在展开面板中
- 新增「卡片摘要」字段（iOS 任务卡片显示文案）
- 「配图/风格备注」折叠到次级展开区减少干扰
- DEFAULT_CONFIG 同步新增 card_summary 字段

commit.message:
Fix TAM+e pronoun chunking and directional handling

Core fix: 'e' after TAM markers (ua, na, sa, o loo) is the 2nd person
singular pronoun "you", not a function word that breaks chunks. Before:
  ua | e silafia a'u → stranded TAM
After:
  ua e silafia a'u → "you have known me" (one chunk)
  o loo e silafia a'u → "you know me" (progressive, one chunk)

Specific changes:
- Added TAM+e pronoun exception in 'e' handler: when prev word is a TAM
  marker (ua/na/sa/ole'a/olo'o/oloo/loo), e stays with the chunk
- Added 'loo'/'oloo' to all _FUNC_E/_FUNC_VOC/_FUNC_VOC2 sets to prevent
  'o loo e' from being treated as vocative pattern
- Added 'o loo' recognition in VSO rule (multi-word TAM check)
- Added TAM-aware exception in forced-break-on-function-word rule: don't
  break directionals/pronouns (mai, atu, ia, te, a'u, etc.) in TAM chunks
- Added 'te' and 'ia' to VSO directional exceptions (pronoun + complement)
- Fixed 'mai' preposition handler: don't break in TAM-initiated phrases

Vocabulary additions:
- mamoe (sheep), fafasi (slaughter), sisila (look/gaze/watch),
  tofotofoina (tested/tried/examined), siliva (silver)
- Fixed silafia: 'seen' → 'know/known/perceived', removed duplicate
- Updated sili: 'most' → 'most/best/greatest'
- New WHOLE_PHRASES: o loo, o loo e, mea e ai, o e

Cache bumped to v97.

feat: Citing Author Disambiguation — 4-phase system to resolve abbreviated Scholar names

Phase 1: Extract Scholar user IDs from Citation.author_profiles JSON during
harvest. Dedup key changed from name_normalized alone to (name_normalized,
scholar_user_id), preventing false aggregation of different scholars sharing
abbreviated names like "Y Wang". Backfill endpoint for existing data.

Phase 2: Citing Authors tab enriched with full names, affiliations, and
disambiguation badges (Ambiguous/ID'd/AI) via batch Author + ScholarAuthorProfile
queries.

Phase 3: LLM clustering endpoint — Haiku-powered paper grouping by topic
coherence, co-author overlap, and venue patterns for ambiguous authors.
Background async task with author_disambiguation_runs table.

Phase 4: AI Evaluate variant-ratio filter skips names with >3 distinct Scholar
profiles and >5 papers, preventing evaluation of fictional aggregate authors.

commit.message:
Fix GPT-5.4 channel analysis parse failures with JSON mode + robust fallbacks (#313)

The "Failed to parse channel analysis response" error persisted despite
prior fixes (#310, #312) because GPT-5.4 with xhigh reasoning would
inconsistently format its output and exhaust token budgets.

Three-pronged fix:
- Add response_format: json_object to force valid JSON at the API level
- Increase max_completion_tokens from 16384 to 32768 for reasoning headroom
- Replace single-pass parsing with 4-strategy pipeline (direct parse,
  fence extraction, brace-depth matching, JSON repair)

Also adds auto-retry without response_format if the model rejects it,
early truncation detection with token diagnostics, and an attemptJsonRepair
utility for recovering truncated JSON responses.

Co-authored-by: Claude <claude@anthropic.com>
